title: Tragacantha agraniotii
taxonomy: Tragacantha agraniotii is a species of plant that belongs to the family Fabaceae. This classification places it within the order Fabales, along with other legume species. At the kingdom level, Tragacantha agraniotii is classified as Plantae, signifying its status as a plant. Within the kingdom Plantae, it is further classified as a member of the division Magnoliophyta, representing flowering plants. At the class level, it is classified as Magnoliopsida, indicating its position within the flowering plants.

intro:
There is not enough information available about Tragacantha agraniotii in English. However, based on information available about Tragacanth (Astragalus gummifer), I can provide a general paragraph about this plant. 

Tragacanth, also known as Tragacantha agraniotii, is a type of plant that belongs to the legume family and is commonly found in the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. The health benefits of Tragacanth include its ability to soothe digestive issues, treat diarrhea, and serve as a natural emollient. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include gum tragacanth, which is a complex mixture of polysaccharides and glycoproteins, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include lozenges, ointments, and suspensions, which are used to treat a variety of ailments, including coughs and sore throats.
